entry.title: Nimbus Simulation Studios Further Teases 707 for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Nimbus Simulation Studios has once again come out on Facebook to share some more images of their upcoming 707 project for X-Plane 12. The previews showcase the aircraft on the ground and in the air around Albuquerque. We still do not know much else about the project, but the team says that they are about 95% done with the external model, texturing and animations. They also added that another preview focusing on the cockpit will be shared sometime next week (13APR-19APR). We will keep an eye on progress! ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates TriStar Airliner for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently updated their rendition of the TriStar Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, bringing a further round of fixes and improvements across core systems. Version 1.0.2 focuses on resolving key areas, including INS behavior, autopilot and flight director logic, ILS functionality, and various cockpit interactions, as well as general stability and usability enhancements. The complete changelog follows below: ‍ V1.0.2SYSTEMS ⚙️ ART 🎨 PFB📜 SOUNDS🔊 The add-on is available in the Marketplace for $49.99 If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: SoFly Updates Cardiff Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. SoFly has recently issued an update to their rendition of Cardiff Airport (EGFF) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, adding animated 3D people, performance enhancements, fixes to photogrammetry issues, and more. ​ Update v1.2.0 brings a major revamp of their scenery product, with improved performance across the board, a smaller file size, mitigation of all photogrammetry issues, removal of ground bumps/lumps, animated 3D passengers, and a custom GSX profile. The complete changelog follows below: It’s available on Contrail for roughly $12.63 (30% off), requiring at least 2.92 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: MK Studios Updates Porto Airport V2 for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. MK Studios has recently issued an update to their newly released Porto Airport (LPPR) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, bringing AFCAD fixes, minor tweaks, and more. Update v2.0.1 - the first so far since its release -brings AFCAD fixes, fixes certain missing procedures, adds the ground frequency, fixes the departures sign, adds custom buildings near runway 17, and adds lights to the train station. ​The scenery is available on Contrail for roughly $20.44, and V1 owners are entitled to a 20% discount coupon. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Aerosoft Updates Düsseldorf Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft has recently issued an update to their rendition of Düsseldorf Airport (EDDL) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, adding new buildings, AFCAD adjustments, terrain fixes, and more. Update v1.0.3 adds the new fire station, new solar farms to the north of the runway, reworks ground markings to match the current layout, adjusts AFCAD parkings and taxiway paths, changes some service vehicle textures, changes taxisigns to the current layout, fixes a terrain glitch on the southeast apron, and tweaks the ground orthoimagery. It’s available on Aerosoft’s shop for roughly $15.99 (20% off), requiring at least 4.74 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: LAND3 Simulations Updates vRAAS for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. LAND3 Simulations has recently issued a major update to their vRAAS add-on for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, adding a realistic voice pack, ROW/ROPS (Airbus) & RSAT (Boeing), multiple fixes, and more. Update v1.3.0 brings a new realistic voice pack, ROW/ROPS (Airbus) and RSAT (Boeing), “Autobrake OFF” callout on Airbus, adds a debug page, improves the timing for distance remaining callouts and altimeter setting tolerances, disables the 1500m and 1200m callouts by default, fixes the auto-start not working for some users, and mitigates the audio device error. It’s available on Contrail for roughly $16.83, requiring at least 338 MB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: FlightFactor's 777-300ER Expansion to Release on Thursday
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. FlightFactor has recently defined a release date for their long-awaited 777-300ER expansion for X-Plane 12, set to land on Thursday (16/04) for $89. The longest member of the 777 family adds to their pre-existing 777v2 lineup, bringing more range, capability, and route variety, costing only $30 to 777V2 owners. It features the GE90-115BL engines as standard, and owners of the engine expansion upgrade will also get access to the GE90-115B. ​ Selected streamers will receive an early access key to showcase the product within the next few days. ​If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: FlightFactor Updates 777v2 for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. FlightFactor has recently issued an update to their 777v2 for X-Plane 12, bringing a variety of new features and fixes in preparation for the 777-300ER release on Thursday. ​ Update 2.5.3 adds the option to disable the analog clock, as per newer MSNs, adds the modern CDU panel and modern compass options, adds a music option, improves CPDLC/ACARS stability and operations, improves support for multi-monitor configurations, and several other fixes. The complete changelog follows below: - added no analog clock option - added modern cdu panel option - added modern compass option - added music option - improved CPDLC/ACARS stability/operations - improved multi-monitor configurations support - fine tuned backlights/instruments/displays brt things according to the references from real plane - fixed cases where closed OS popup displays appear empty after next plane loading - fixed ACARS systems not working after first successful request on macOS/Linux - fixed alternate switching on of C1/C2 demand pumps during movement Flaps - fixed issue in OPT when hg and hPa determined incorrectly - fixed WX RDR panel tooltips text - fixed various 2D window popups (related to procedures, crew operations and tooltips) having wrong position in specific multi-monitor configurations - fixed Available and Operational numbers swapped on EFB MEL page - fixed possible fmc soft crash when the vnav cdu crz page is active and spd limit mode is active - fixed fpa is displayed on the ground on des vnav page - fixed can't access cockpit via gate - updated the remote CDU, a new generation added ‍ The add-on is available on the X-Plane.Org Store for roughly $89.00, requiring at least 7 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Official Trailer for the FlightFactor 777-300ER Now Out
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Ahead of the anticipated product launch tomorrow, Apr. 16, FlightFactor has now released the official trailer movie for their Boeing 777-300ER, coming to X-Plane 12. Uploaded via the X-Plane.org Store YouTube Channel, and produced by Fly777X Aviation, we see what is likely very close to a finished release candidate flying on the big screen. The 777-300ER is part of FlightFactor’s 777V2 series for X-Plane 12, aiming to rebuild their original 777 line-up from scratch, in very high detail. The release cycle began with the 777-200ER last year, followed by the 777F, with the long-awaited 300ER now imminent as well. It is scheduled to release tomorrow, Thursday Apr. 16, although FlightFactor has not yet confirmed a specific release time. For a closer look at the aircraft, you can take a look at one of the numerous pre-release streams broadcast over the last few days by several content creators. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FlightFactor Releases 777-300ER v2 for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. FlightFactor has recently released their long-awaited 777-300ER v2 for X-Plane 12, adding to their next-generation 777 lineup and bringing more range, seats, and route variety to the mix. It brings a high-fidelity rendition of the 77W, with custom aerodynamics and systems, meeting real-life standards as per the FCOM, with a flight model that is within 95 to 98% accurate on standard certification tests (tested by real pilots), alongside a wide list of features: ✅ Unparalleled Realism 🔹 Every aspect of this model replicates the real-world Boeing 777, with aerodynamics and systems meticulously crafted to meet the rigorous standards of the FCOM.🔹 Developed with insights from technical manuals, maintenance guides, and certified pilots—including FlightFactor’s CEO, a CFI and 777-rated pilot. ✅ Highly Accurate🔹 Proprietary flight model within 2%-5% accuracy on standard certification tests, tested with real pilots.🔹 Fully simulated weight and balance, including cabin configurations, oil, filters, water, and waste. ✅ Stunning Exterior & Interior🔹 True-to-life dimensions based on official Boeing exterior drawings.🔹 Highly detailed exterior modeling with ultra-high-resolution textures.🔹 Realistic 3D cockpit with high-resolution visuals.🔹 Fully modeled passenger cabin with adjustable seating configurations.🔹 Real working oxygen masks, dynamic sun-reactive window blinds, and fully accessible compartments. ✅ Authentic Aircraft Systems🔹 Fully simulated electrical, hydraulic, fuel, and air systems.🔹 Navigation systems: GPS, VOR, DME, ADF, COM, ILS.🔹 TCAS v7 with full collision avoidance functionality.🔹 Standby instruments (Classic STBY & ISFD) and working overhead circuit breakers.🔹 Advanced flight control system with:🔸 Three Primary Flight Computers (PFC)🔸 Four Actuator Control Electronics (ACE)🔸 Normal, Secondary, and Direct control modes ✅ New Weather Radar ✅ EFIS & Flight Computers🔹 Six LCD displays with full auto/manual source switching logic.🔹 Integrated checklist pages, COMM pages, and all synoptic displays.🔹 Dual Flight Management Computers (FMC) and three Control Display Units (CDU).🔹 Three Autopilot Flight Director Systems (AFDS) with full GPWS, RAAS, and look-ahead terrain alerting.🔹 Weather radar with realistic beam effects and ground clutter simulation. ✅ Ground Services🔹 Interactive pushback truck controllable via joystick.🔹 Passenger bus, stairs, or direct gate boarding.🔹 Functional fuel truck, de-icing truck, GPU, and ground-start units. 🔹Interactive Automatic Procedures allows you to imitate the standard operating procedures of an airliner. 🔹Command, monitor, and interact with the First Officer during standard normal and abnormal procedures ✅ Immersive Audio Experience🔹 Fully proprietary sound engine with hundreds of real aircraft recordings.🔹 3D stereo engine sounds with realistic cabin and airport environment audio.🔹 Interactive crew communication, in-flight announcements, and FO voice tooltips. ✅ Innovative Cockpit Interaction🔹 Multiple modes for cockpit interaction, including mouse wheel and right-button support.🔹 Click on controls to display manual tooltips.🔹 Over 2,000 custom click zones with assignable commands. ✅ Realistic Crew & Checklist System🔹 Fully interactive checklists with automatic action detection.🔹 Four crew voice characters with AI-driven preflight briefings.🔹 FO-assisted procedures, callouts, and voice-controlled interactions. ✅ Failure & Maintenance System🔹 Nearly 1,000 simulated failures with full training scenarios.🔹 Persistent wear and tear system—aircraft condition carries over between flights. ✅ Situation Save & Pre-Set Scenarios🔹 Save your flight at any stage and resume later.🔹 Preconfigured training scenarios and multiple cold-and-dark startup options. ✅ Advanced Electronic Flight Bag (EFB)🔹 Integrated Navigraph charts and airport moving maps.🔹 SimBrief integration for detailed flight planning.🔹 Interactive navlog and weight/balance optimization tools. ✅ Exclusive Special Effects🔹 Multi-layer dynamic glass reflections.🔹 Screen glow effects and weather-enhanced wind shear simulation. ✅ FlightFactor Airport Visual System (AVS)✅ High-Resolution Boeing 777v2 Poster (13,380 x 22,850 resolution, includes system schematics) ✅ New Camera Systems 🔹 allows viewing of landing gears while taxing✅ Liveries Included:🔹 Air France NC🔹 British Airways🔹 Emirates🔹 FlightFactor House🔹 KLM OC🔹 United NC🔹 Qatar F1 It’s available on the X-Plane.Org Store for $89 (standalone), $149 (bundle), and $30 to upgrade from the 777v2. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Fenix Simulations Updates A320 Lineup for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Fenix Simulations has recently issued an update to their A320 lineup for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, addressing issues with SU5 (2024) and the charts app, and bringing the Navdata cycle to the latest free cycle (2503). Update 2.4.0.4720 is primarily a hotfix, addressing incompatibilities with SU5 (2024 only), fixing the charts app so it displays a map for users without an active Navigraph subscription, and updating the Navdata cycle to the latest free one (2503). The complete changelog follows below: ‍2.4.0.4720 Avionics and Systems Art and Sound EFB The A320 Lineup is available in their store, starting from $67.62. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: FlightSimExpo 2026 Registration Opens April 18, Microsoft in attendance
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. FlightSimExpo 2026 is just two months away, and the organisers have now provided an update on what attendees can expect this summer, including registration timelines and tours, early exhibitor confirmations, and on-site activities. Most notably, attendee registration for tours and experiences are set to open this weekend, two phases. FSA Captains get priority access from 12:00 CDT on April 18, while general attendees can begin booking 24 hours later. These add-on experiences are positioned as a core part of the weekend, and this year’s lineup leans heavily into both aviation access and social events. Attendees can book an airside bus tour of Minneapolis–Saint Paul International Airport, a guided visit to Delta’s Technical Operations facilities, and a range of other activities, including a 5K “fun run”, a brewery walking tour through downtown Saint Paul, and a group trip to the Mall of America. You can find more information on everything related to this at https://flightsimexpo.com/tours. In May, a second wave of sign-ups for exhibit hall activities will open. The planned activities in the exhibit hall include: You can find more information on the exhibit hall signups at flightsimexpo.com/signups. On the exhibitor side, during the recent developer livestream, Microsoft confirmed that the Microsoft Flight Simulator team will be present on the show floor. They will be part of a wider exhibitor lineup expected to exceed 60 participants, although the full list has not yet been released. This reveal is coming later this month, and according to the organisers, it has already been filmed on location in Saint Paul. FlightSimExpo 2026 takes place June 12–14 at the Saint Paul RiverCentre, located roughly 8 miles from MSP airport. The location offers over 130 direct routes to MSP and rail connections via Amtrak’s Borealis and Empire Builder services. If you have not yet booked a ticket, it would be wise to do this now, as tickets are selling out. The discounted $ 159-per-night hotel rate at the InterContinental is expected to sell out quickly, and prices may rise further depending on external factors, including NHL playoff activity in the same area. On-site, #FSExpoFriday will again host major product announcements before the exhibit hall opens for the evening, followed by full-day access across Saturday and Sunday. Alongside this, attendees can expect seminars, Q&A sessions, workshops, and panel discussions running throughout the weekend. There are also some new additions, premiering at this year’s event. One of them is the FlightSimExpo Combat Arena, which introduces a more interactive, competitive element, featuring air-combat-style challenges and team-based activities. Beyond aviation, the show floor will feature other simulation sectors, such as racing and rail. flightsimexpo.com/schedule The Media Partner Lounge is set to return, supported by several community creators, including Closed Traffic Podcast, FactualGull, Fly With Alyson, Mr Squirrel Lee, and Will Shaw. It will again serve as a central meeting point for media, creators, and attendees. There is also an option for partners to host meet-and-greet sessions and feature branding in the space, with a registration deadline of May 1. A full weekend pass costs $85, and single-day tickets range from $35 to $55 depending on the day. As always, FSA Captains receive a 20% discount and priority access to certain activities. For those not attending in person, an online access option will again be available, offering live and on-demand access to seminars, a virtual exhibit hall walkthrough, and community access via Discord. ‍ Threshold will be in attendance at the event, and we will keep you updated on everything that happens, leading up to and during FlightSimExpo 2026. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Microsoft Hosts April 2026 Developer Stream, incl. SU5, AAU4, WU21 and more
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Microsoft has published its April 2026 developer update, following a lengthy livestream. It was hosted on Twitch, now available on YouTube as well. Sim Update 5 is currently targeting release between Apr. 20 and Apr. 24, pending resolution of a small number of remaining issues. The update brings PSVR2 support on PlayStation 5, the first batch of third-party add-ons on the PS5 marketplace, and a long-awaited weather radar with tilt functionality. Alongside that, work on improving performance has continued, with further optimisation of multi-threading, memory allocation, and heavy aircraft scenarios. A highlight of this update is an updated airflow simulation. This introduces improved turbulence modelling, better wake interaction, and more realistic behaviour inside clouds, including density changes caused by thermals. Visualisation tools have been expanded, now using colour grading to indicate airflow strength. Aircraft and Avionics Update 4 will release alongside SU5, bringing several aircraft up to native MSFS 2024 standards. This includes the ATR 42/72, Antonov An-2, Fokker F.VII, Latécoère 631, and Savoia-Marchetti S.55, with further updates planned. These upgrades focus on improved system depth, visuals, and integration with newer sim features. Looking slightly ahead, the next World Update will focus on Australia, significantly expanding coverage here. Included will be over 30 new areas of interest, multiple handcrafted airports including Broome and Coober Pedy, and a set of new activities. Local Legend 24 will be releasing alongside this update, the CAC Boomerang, a WWII aircraft developed by iniBuilds. Two aircraft releases are planned. The Piper M600, developed by FSReborn, will be released as Expert Series 02 in June. Later in the year, the Embraer Praetor 600 will follow as Expert Series 03. Microsoft also said bug fixing remains the top focus, described internally as a “zero bug initiative”, even if that is not realistically achievable. Marketplace improvements are also planned, with curated content, gifting support, and a more controlled rollout on PlayStation, all in the pipeline. Microsoft will also be in attendance at FlightSimExpo 2026. Read more about that here. ‍ Watch the full developer stream on Twitch and YouTube. Read it in the written format here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: PMDG Releases High Detail Update for 737-600, Post-Release Update for 737-700HDU
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. PMDG has released its latest round of updates for the 737NG series, with the 737-600 now receiving the awaited High Detail Update, while the 737-700 has received a post-release patch with additional visual changes, system improvements, and some teething fixes. As headlined, the 737-600 is now part of the lineup with the High Detail Update, something PMDG first outlined back in November 2025 and later expanded in March. The update includes a rework of the aircraft model, with better geometry, improved textures and more. PMDG says that the changes go deep into the model, with tens of thousands of additional rivets, screws, and fasteners modelled, alongside updated surface mapping, improved animations on external panels and hatches, and reworked ground equipment. Further features are updates to the cockpit, cabin, cargo areas, and gear bays, built using the MSFS 2024 SDK. Alongside the 737-600 update, both the 737-600 and 737-700 have gotten a set of system and stability improvements. These include improved WASM stability, performance improvements in tablet map rendering, and the integration of BeyondATC datalink capability. As usual, the full list of changes is available through the PMDG Operations Center. A post-release update for the 737-700 has already been submitted to the MSFS Marketplace, also available directly through PMDG’s own store and the Operations Center. Updates for the 737-900 and 737-800 are planned next, in that order, though no exact timeline has been provided yet. Because of the scale of changes introduced with the High Detail Update, all official liveries distributed via the Operations Center have been updated. Third-party liveries created prior to HDU may require adjustments from their authors, and compatibility can vary depending on how they were built. ‍ The update is available now via the PMDG Operations Center, with a Marketplace release to follow once Microsoft completes its intake process. ‍ Full update post on PMDG’s forums. ‍ Separately, but worth noting, PMDG has also put out a call for real-world input, seeking individuals who have worked as crew on the Boeing 747-100 or other early 747 “classic” variants. Contact them here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Fly X Simulations Releases Belfast City Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Fly X Simulations has released its rendition of George Best Belfast City Airport (EGAC), a compact airport in Northern Ireland, for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024. The scenery is now available via the iniBuilds store. Located a few minutes out from central Belfast, EGAC is an important regional gateway, with the airlines operating there including Aer Lingus, British Airways, easyJet, and Loganair, serving routes all across the UK and to nearby European destinations. The release includes a full, high-detail airport model of the terminal and its interior, with custom PBR ground textures and accurate stand markings, and hand-placed clutter, including service vehicles and static cars. ‍ The package also comes with a custom GSX profile by default. ‍ Fly X Simulations’ Belfast City Airport is now available on the iniBuilds store, priced at $16.27 USD, for both MSFS 2020 and 2024. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: ‍Dreamflight Studios Provides Update on Teterboro V2, Guanacaste V2
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Dreamflight Studios has provided an update on two of its airport projects, starting with a first proper look at Teterboro V2, followed by a clarification on the status of Guanacaste V2, both of which appear to have grown well beyond their original scope. Starting with Teterboro, the developer has revealed that a full V2 rebuild is on the way, marking six years since the original release. Rather than iterating on the existing version, Dreamflight has rebuilt the airport entirely from the ground up, aiming to bring it in line with its current standards. It will include upgraded textures, reworked models, and a more cohesive overall build across the airport. Teterboro’s role as a major business aviation hub just outside New York City is also worth mentioning, with Dreamflight highlighting seven fully recreated FBOs, each with detailed interiors. We do not yet have a release date for the project, but it looks to be shaping up nicely. You can watch a preview stream of the scenery below: In a separate post, Dreamflight addressed MRLBv2, confirming that the project is still in active development after a period of silence. What was initially planned as a smaller update has since evolved into a full rebuild, after the team revisited the original assets and found they no longer met the quality level of their newer work. This shift explains Dreamflight’s silence, with the developer acknowledging the communication gap while noting that progress is now well underway. According to Dreamflight, MRLBv2 is “not far away” and will be released as a free update for existing users. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!
